task_name: Ureter Identification (Right) — VirtaMed ED Surgical Simulation Tutorial

frame_analyses.description: VirtaMed ED splash screen and title card showing 'Ureter Identification Right'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational module introduction — no performance task underway

frame_analyses.description: Instructional overlay: 'Palpate ureter at right pelvic brim'; uterine manipulator visible in training model; laparoscopic view of pelvic anatomy with yellow structures (bowel) and pink structures (reproductive organs)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narrated instruction with text guidance and anatomical landmark highlighting. Shows expert hand placement technique for palpating ureter.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic instrument (appears to be grasper/dissector) approaching right pelvic anatomy; instruction: 'Identify the ureter provoking peristalsis at the level of the right pelvic brim where it crosses over the iliac vessels.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates careful palpation technique; shows proper instrument angle and approach to avoid vessel injury. Verbal cue emphasizes anatomical landmarks (iliac vessels).

frame_analyses.description: Instructions transition to 'Follow ureter' phase; narrator instructs: 'Follow the course of the ureter into the pouch of Douglas.' Instrument continues palpating and tracing tissue planes.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates dissection technique and tissue handling. Shows smooth, controlled instrument movement along anatomical structure.

frame_analyses.description: Both laparoscopic instruments visible; fine dissection continuing along right pelvic sidewall. Anatomy becomes clearer as peritoneum is mobilized.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Two-handed laparoscopic technique demonstrated; coordinated grasping and retracting to expose ureter course.

frame_analyses.description: Continued tracing of ureter into pouch of Douglas; anatomy now well-visualized with pink tissue (peritoneum) retracted laterally.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth dissection, no excessive trauma to surrounding structures; demonstrates patient, systematic approach to anatomical identification.

frame_analyses.description: Ureter course fully delineated into pouch of Douglas; laparoscopic view now wide, showing full extent of dissection from pelvic brim to cul-de-sac.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Complete anatomical visualization achieved; demonstrates importance of gentle, systematic dissection for safe identification.

frame_analyses.description: Instruction changes to 'Activate LED stents'; menu appears on right side showing assistant options (Hold camera position, Activate LED Stent, Show guidance hints). Instruction reads: 'Activate LED stents by pressing the "Activate led stent" button to highlight the course of the ureters.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Interactive learning feature — demonstrates use of simulation tools to confirm anatomical identification. LED stent activation is a teaching aid, not a clinical technique.

frame_analyses.description: LED stent visualization activated; bright pink/fluorescent highlight now visible along entire ureter course from pelvic brim to pouch of Douglas.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows correct anatomical path of right ureter for reference and self-assessment.

frame_analyses.description: LED stent highlighting remains visible; broader anatomical view showing relationship of ureter to surrounding structures (bowel, peritoneal reflection).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational reinforcement of anatomical landmarks and spatial relationships.

frame_analyses.description: Final instruction appears: 'Remove the instruments'; laparoscopic view shows anatomical structures with LED stent still highlighted.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Module conclusion phase — demonstrates safe instrument withdrawal protocol.

frame_analyses.description: VirtaMed ED branding and contact information displayed (sales@virtamed.com); module conclusion.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: End-of-module screen; instructional content fully completed.

confidence_rationale: This is clearly instructional/educational content from VirtaMed, not a performance-based FLS task. No scoring is applicable. The video demonstrates expert technique for ureteral identification in a surgical simulation environment with narrated guidance, anatomical overlays, and interactive learning tools. No trainee performance to evaluate.
technique_summary: This is a structured surgical simulation tutorial demonstrating the identification and dissection of the right ureter in a pelvic anatomy trainer. The module uses laparoscopic simulation with expert-level technique, anatomical landmarks, interactive LED stent visualization, and step-by-step verbal guidance. This is educational content, not an FLS performance task.

demonstrated_techniques: Palpation of ureter at right pelvic brim
Identification of ureter by peristalsis provocation
Recognition of iliac vessel crossing as anatomical landmark
Gentle dissection along tissue planes using two-handed laparoscopic technique
Systematic tracing of ureter from pelvic brim into pouch of Douglas
Safe instrument handling to avoid vascular injury
Use of retraction and counter-retraction for visualization
verbal_cues: Palpate ureter at right pelvic brim
Identify the ureter provoking peristalsis at the level of the right pelvic brim where it crosses over the iliac vessels
Follow the course of the ureter into the pouch of Douglas
Activate LED stents by pressing the 'Activate led stent' button to highlight the course of the ureters
Safely remove the instruments and the laparoscope from the patient to finish
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding excessive traction on peritoneum (demonstrated by gentle dissection technique)
Preventing vascular injury by respecting anatomical planes around iliac vessels
Careful palpation without crushing ureter tissue
Systematic approach rather than random probing

improvement_suggestions: Not applicable — this is instructional content, not a performance evaluation
